Rust vs Go for Backend Services
Both are strong — here's how they compare:
Rust wins on raw throughput; zero-cost abstractions,
Go compiles in seconds, ships a single binary,
stdlib covers most backend needs out of the box.
Go goroutines are simpler; Rust async/await prevents
Go has mature HTTP/gRPC tooling; Rust's Axum and
Actix are production-grade and growing fast.
Go for fast iteration and team scale;
Rust for latency-critical or memory-constrained services.
